title: Podman Fails to Mount Volume in `/tmp` on macOS
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am currently using Podman version 4.1.1.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

The following 2 commands fail:

```bash
$ podman run --rm -it -v /tmp:/tmp ubuntu:20.04 bash
$ podman run --rm -it -v /private/tmp:/tmp ubuntu:20.04 bash
```

This is because `/tmp` is a symlink to `/private/tmp` on macOS. However, the following works fine:

```bash
$ podman run --rm -it -v /Users:/Users ubuntu:20.04 bash
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Podman reports the following:

```text
Error: statfs /tmp: no such file or directory
Error: statfs /private/tmp: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman should be able to mount tmp directories on macOS, whether if using the canonicalized path (`/private/tmp`) or just using `/tmp`. In case additional debugging information is required, the podman info, version, and brew info for the install is available [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14492). The macOS version is 10.15.7 (Catalina).

comments.body:
Could you try the following first:

podman machine init -v /tmp:/tmp -v /private:/private 

We are only defaulting to mounting $HOME into the VM at this time, other directories would need to be added separately.  These directories can be specified in containers.conf.

I don't have access to my macOS computer today, but this is almost certainly the issue. Can we have a change in error message then, to make it easier for users to understand? Rather than say, "Error: statfs $dir: no such file or directory", it could say, "Error: $dir is not mounted in the VM, you must add the directory when running `podman machine init`".

It would make it much clearer that this is not a bug, and an implementation detail.

Podman service in the VM has no way of knowing what the user was attempting to do, and does not even realize that it is in a VM. It is reporting the error. Also know way to differentiate a typo from what the user intended.
